strengths: Nearly two decades of hands-on development experience — far beyond the 6+ year requirement — including recent full-stack TypeScript/Node.js/React product work at Optilyz and re:cinq
Direct AI-agent development experience at re:cinq, building MCP (Model Context Protocol) code generators and frameworks for agentic workflows — a rare, explicitly required skill
Deep testing and craftsmanship background: TDD, pairing, CI/CD, trunk-based development at RIDE, authored test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and taught multi-module testing courses
concerns: Nest.js is not explicitly listed, though extensive Node.js backend experience suggests a short ramp-up
Vitest/Jest/Playwright are not named directly; documented testing expertise centers on D-language tooling and Selenium
Kotlin is absent and Java experience appears dated (Altom, 2016–2018), leaving the secondary JVM stack thinner

key_factors.factor: AI agent and MCP tooling experience

response.justification: This candidate is a strong match for the role's core: end-to-end feature delivery in TypeScript/Node.js/React, backed by nearly twenty years of professional experience spanning web, mobile, and high-throughput backend systems. They uniquely satisfy the hardest requirement — AI agent development — through current work on MCP code generators and agentic-workflow frameworks at re:cinq. Their polyglot range (TypeScript, Java, C/C++, D, PHP, Swift) fits the multi-language expectation, and their experience with MongoDB, MySQL, Docker, Kubernetes, and AWS CDK covers the data and cloud requirements. A demonstrated craftsmanship ethos — TDD, CI/CD, code katas, teaching, and open-source test tooling — aligns directly with the role's emphasis on code quality, reviews, and process improvement, making the missing Nest.js/Vitest line items a matter of tooling familiarity rather than capability.
